input: What is the waiting period for cataract surgery?
expected_output: Cataract surgery is excluded during the first 24 months of coverage under the policy.
retrieval_context: 3.1 Cataract surgery is excluded during the first 24 months of coverage.
3.2 Other ophthalmology procedures require a 12-month waiting period.

input: Is maternity covered under the policy?
expected_output: Maternity expenses are covered after a waiting period of 9 months. Normal delivery is covered up to INR 25,000 and Caesarean up to INR 50,000.
retrieval_context: 3.2 Maternity expenses are covered after a waiting period of 9 months.
Normal delivery: up to INR 25,000. Caesarean: up to INR 50,000.

input: What is the grace period for premium payment?
expected_output: A grace period of 30 days is provided for premium payment after the due date. Coverage continues during the grace period.
retrieval_context: Section 5.1: A grace period of 30 days is allowed for renewal premium payments.
During the grace period, the policy shall remain in force.

expected_output: Pre-existing diseases are covered after a waiting period of 48 months of continuous coverage under the policy.
retrieval_context: 4.1 Pre-existing diseases shall be covered after 48 months of continuous coverage.
A pre-existing disease is defined as any condition diagnosed or treated in the 48 months prior to policy inception.

input: What is the room rent sub-limit?
expected_output: Room rent is limited to 1% of the sum insured per day for a single private AC room.
retrieval_context: Section 6.2: Room rent is capped at 1% of the sum insured per day.
Intensive Care Unit charges are limited to 2% of the sum insured per day.

expected_output: AYUSH treatment is covered up to the sum insured when taken in a government-recognised AYUSH hospital or institute.
retrieval_context: Section 7.4: Expenses for Ayurveda, Yoga, Unani, Siddha, and Homeopathy treatments are covered.
Coverage applies when treatment is received in a government-recognised AYUSH hospital.

input: What is the ambulance charge limit?
expected_output: Ambulance charges are covered up to INR 3,000 per hospitalisation.
retrieval_context: Section 8.1: Road ambulance charges are covered up to INR 3,000 per hospitalisation event.
Air ambulance is not covered under the standard policy.

expected_output: Injuries sustained while participating in adventure sports are excluded from coverage.
retrieval_context: Section 9.3: The policy excludes claims arising from participation in hazardous or adventure sports activities.
Examples include mountaineering, bungee jumping, skydiving, and motor racing.

expected_output: A co-payment of 10% of the admissible claim amount is applicable for insured persons above 60 years of age.
retrieval_context: Section 10.1: A co-pay of 10% shall be borne by the insured for every admissible claim.
This co-payment clause applies to insured persons who are 60 years of age or older.

input: How long does the insurer take to settle a claim?
expected_output: The insurer will settle or reject a claim within 30 days of receiving all required documents.
retrieval_context: Section 11.2: Upon receipt of all required claim documents, the insurer shall settle or reject the claim within 30 days.
Delayed settlement attracts interest at the bank rate plus 2% per annum.

expected_output: Domiciliary hospitalisation is covered for a minimum period of 3 days, provided the condition requires hospitalisation but the patient cannot be moved to a hospital.
retrieval_context: Section 12.1: Domiciliary hospitalisation expenses are covered for a minimum continuous period of 3 days.
Coverage applies when the patient's condition requires hospitalisation but admission is not possible due to medical reasons.

input: What surgeries are excluded during the first year?
expected_output: During the first year of the policy, surgeries for hernia, cataracts, joint replacement, and sinusitis are excluded.
retrieval_context: Section 13.1 First-year exclusions: hernia, cataract, joint replacement, sinusitis, and varicose veins.
These exclusions apply only during the first 12 months and are covered thereafter unless a specific waiting period applies.

expected_output: Dental treatment expenses are excluded unless arising from an accident causing bodily injury.
retrieval_context: Section 9.5: Dental treatment, including dental surgery, is excluded from coverage.
An exception applies when dental injury is caused by a direct accident resulting in physical trauma.

expected_output: The insured may cancel the policy within 15 days of receiving the policy document if they disagree with the terms, and receive a proportionate refund of premium.
retrieval_context: Section 14.1: A free-look period of 15 days is available from the date of receipt of the policy.
On cancellation during the free-look period, the insurer shall refund premium after deducting proportionate risk premium.

input: Is mental illness covered under the policy?
expected_output: Mental illness hospitalisation is covered under the policy in compliance with the Mental Healthcare Act, 2017.
retrieval_context: Section 15.2: Coverage for mental illness is provided as required by the Mental Healthcare Act, 2017.
Inpatient psychiatric treatment is covered up to the sum insured on the same terms as physical illness.
